Yarn 4 is the intended modern tooling â€” pinned per-project via `packageManager`, faster installs, plugin system, better workspace story for the future.

title: Acceptance criteria
description:
- `frontend/package.json` has `"packageManager": "yarn@4.x.y"` pinned to a specific version
- `frontend/.yarnrc.yml` exists with `nodeLinker: node-modules` (no PnP at MVP â€” fewer Vite/Mantine plugin compatibility surprises)
- `frontend/yarn.lock` committed; `frontend/package-lock.json` removed
- `frontend/Dockerfile` switched from `npm ci`/`npm install` to `corepack enable && yarn install --immutable`
- `frontend/.dockerignore` ignores `.yarn/cache` and `.yarn/install-state.gz` if `nodeLinker: node-modules` is used
- Root `.gitignore` adds yarn local state: `.yarn/install-state.gz`, `.pnp.*`, `.yarn/cache/` (we'll commit `.yarn/releases/` and `.yarn/plugins/` if used)
- `package.json` scripts unchanged (`yarn dev`, `yarn build` invoke them the same way)

title: Frontend skeleton + theme
description:
## Goal
Bootstrap a Vite + React + Mantine app that calls /health.

## Acceptance criteria
- `frontend/` initialised with Vite (React + TypeScript template)
- Mantine v7 installed (`@mantine/core`, `@mantine/hooks`, `@emotion/react`)
- `frontend/src/theme.ts` exports the ScorePlay theme from CLAUDE.md Â§6
- `MantineProvider` wraps the app with the theme + `defaultColorScheme="dark"`
- Page calls `/api/health` (via Vite dev proxy â†’ backend `:8080/health`), displays response
- `frontend/Dockerfile` multi-stage: build with Node, serve with nginx on port 80
- Vite proxy configured so `/api/*` â†’ `http://backend:8080/*` in dev

## Verification
`docker build -t loggingstudio-frontend ./frontend` succeeds.
Dev mode: `pnpm dev` (or `npm run dev`) inside frontend/ serves at `:5173` and the page renders with the dark theme.
notes: Vite + React + Mantine v7 + ScorePlay dark theme. App.tsx fetches /api/health. Dev proxy via VITE_PROXY_TARGET (default localhost:8080). Multi-stage Dockerfile: node:20-alpine build â†’ nginx:1.27-alpine serve, nginx proxies /api â†’ backend:8080. Verified: docker build OK, npm run build OK, vite dev serves :5173 with correct index.html. Visual dark-theme render needs human eyes.

title: Verification
description:
From clean checkout: `docker compose up --build` brings all three services up.
`curl localhost:8080/health` returns 200.
Browser at `localhost:5173` shows the frontend, which displays the /health response.
`docker compose down` shuts cleanly. `pgdata/` is preserved on disk.
close_reason: merged into loggingstudio-bwj (Compose + wiring); seed split error

title: Compose + wiring
description:
## Goal
One `docker compose up --build` brings up the full stack.

## Acceptance criteria
- `docker-compose.yml` at repo root with three services:
  - `postgres`: image `pgvector/pgvector:pg16`, env `POSTGRES_USER=loggingstudio`, `POSTGRES_PASSWORD=loggingstudio`, `POSTGRES_DB=loggingstudio`, volume `./pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data`, port `5432` exposed
  - `backend`: built from `./backend`, env `DATABASE_URL=postgres://loggingstudio:loggingstudio@postgres:5432/loggingstudio?sslmode=disable`, depends_on postgres (healthy), volume `./watch:/watch`, port `8080`
  - `frontend`: built from `./frontend`, depends_on backend, port `5173:80`
- Postgres healthcheck so backend waits for db ready
- All services share a network named `loggingstudio`

## Verification
From clean checkout: `docker compose up --build` brings all three services up.
`curl localhost:8080/health` returns 200.
Browser at `localhost:5173` shows the frontend, which displays the /health response.
`docker compose down` shuts cleanly. `pgdata/` is preserved on disk.

title: Root scaffolding
description:
## Goal
Create top-level directory structure for the project.

## Acceptance criteria
- Directories exist: `backend/`, `frontend/`, `shared/`, `watch/`, `pgdata/`
- `pgdata/` and `watch/processed/`, `watch/rejected/` are in `.gitignore`
- `.gitkeep` files in `shared/`, `watch/`, `backend/`, `frontend/` so empty dirs are tracked
- `.gitignore` updated to exclude `pgdata/`, `node_modules/`, `*.log`, `.env`, `.DS_Store`

## Verification
`ls -la` shows the directories. `git status` shows them tracked (via .gitkeep).
notes: Scaffolding dirs (backend/, frontend/, shared/, watch/, pgdata/) created with .gitkeep files. pgdata/ added to .gitignore. Verified via ls + git status.

title: Backend skeleton
description:
## Goal
Bootstrap a minimal Go service that returns a health check.

## Acceptance criteria
- `backend/go.mod` initialised (module name: `github.com/zav1995/loggingstudio/backend`)
- Gin added as a dependency
- `backend/cmd/server/main.go` exposes `GET /health` returning `{"ok": true, "ts": "<iso8601>"}`
- `backend/Dockerfile` (multi-stage: build with `golang:1.22`, run with `alpine` or `distroless`)
- Listens on `:8080`

## Verification
`docker build -t loggingstudio-backend ./backend` succeeds.
`docker run --rm -p 8080:8080 loggingstudio-backend` starts and `curl localhost:8080/health` returns 200 with the expected JSON.
notes: Gin on :8080, /health returns {ok,ts}. Multi-stage Dockerfile (golang:1.22-alpine â†’ alpine:3.20, non-root). Gin pinned to v1.10.0 (v1.12.0 requires Go 1.25). Verified: docker build + run + curl all green.

title: Migrate frontend tooling from npm to yarn 4
description:
## Goal
Replace npm with yarn 4 (Berry, via corepack) as the frontend package manager.

## Why
npm was an unplanned fallback during the frontend skeleton work (loggingstudio-5el) because the local corepack v0.30 has stale signing keys and couldn't fetch pnpm/yarn at install time. Yarn 4 is the intended modern tooling â€” pinned per-project via packageManager, faster installs, plugin system, better workspace story later.

## Acceptance criteria
- frontend/package.json has "packageManager": "yarn@4.x.y" pinned to a specific version
- frontend/.yarnrc.yml exists with nodeLinker: node-modules (no PnP at MVP â€” fewer Vite/Mantine plugin compatibility surprises)
- frontend/yarn.lock committed; frontend/package-lock.json removed
- frontend/Dockerfile switched from npm ci/npm install to corepack enable && yarn install --immutable
- frontend/.dockerignore ignores .yarn/cache and .yarn/install-state.gz
- Root .gitignore adds yarn local state: .yarn/install-state.gz, .pnp.*, .yarn/cache/
- package.json scripts unchanged (yarn dev, yarn build invoke them the same way)

## Pre-requisite
Local corepack must be >= 0.31 (signing-key bundle fix). Run npm i -g corepack@latest first. Note this in README dev-setup later.

## Verification
- docker build -t loggingstudio-frontend ./frontend succeeds
- yarn dev inside frontend/ serves at :5173 and renders the dark-theme health page
- yarn build produces a clean dist/
- docker compose up --build brings the stack up cleanly with no errors
